4.1: Active Microservices RSS RAM: Live Telemetry

diary._comment: diary_list screen AST template. All integer keys validated against HbpConstants.ts. Key ref: '0'=typeId, 'id'=node_id, '2'=children, '3'=behaviors, '4'=content. Action keys: '70'={0:action_type, 1:method_id, 3:route, 4:params}. RPC methods: 112=create_block, 113=create_entry, 114=set_private, 115=generate_from_notes. Action types: 1=RPC_CALL, 2=NAVIGATE, 3=DISMISS, 7=STATE_SYNC.

diary_block_picker._comment: Block picker bottom sheet — 31 block types organized into 8 categories. Each category is a MarkdownEditor label (readonly, caption) + Wrap of Chips. Each Chip fires create_block RPC (method 112). Hydrated at runtime with {{entry_id}}.

4.0: Choose a block type to insert into your diary

diary_editor._comment: diary_editor screen shell. The blocks_list children are populated at runtime by SduiScreenAssembler._assembleDiaryEditor() — not from this template. The 'template' node is cloned per block and the content primitive is injected by SduiBlockRegistry. Toolbar: [back_btn | title (EXPAND) | bolt_chat | add_block_btn].

template._comment: Block wrapper — cloned per DB block by SduiScreenAssembler. Content primitive is replaced by SduiBlockRegistry.buildContentNode(). The drag_handle triggers ReorderableListView drag (action_type=8=DRAG). delete_btn fires delete_block RPC (111).

2._comment: Content placeholder — replaced at runtime by SduiBlockRegistry. typeId + behaviors are set per block.blockType.

v4_container._comment: Universal Container Test Rig. Evaluates nested layouts, borders, colors, padding, and elevations.

4.0:
1. Perform **3 sets of 10 reps** of push-ups.
2. Keep your back completely straight.
3. Breathe out on exertion.

Today I successfully completed the SDUI 4 architectural overhaul. The legacy SDUI-3 system has been fully archived, and all UI routing is now handled by the Node.js WebSocket Orchestrator Engine. Memory ceilings are stable.
